factor.$description: Defines the shrinkage of a component in the density modes. Gets smaller with each density.

treshold.$description: Prevents components getting too small in density modes. Grows with each density.

growth.$description: Size distance between component sizes (e.g. component s + growth factor = component m)

body.$description: Factor for font size decrease based on the previous calculated font size

utility.$description: Factor for font size decrease based on the previous calculated font size

headline.$description: Factor for font size decrease based on the previous calculated font size

body.$description: Factor for font size increase based on the previous calculated font size

utility.$description: Factor for font size increase based on the previous calculated font size

headline.$description: Factor for font size increase based on the previous calculated font size

body.$description: cushion relates to the 2 * 2px (or any other value) concept from https://kittygiraudel.com/2020/05/18/using-calc-to-figure-out-optimal-line-height/

utility.$description: cushion relates to the 2 * 2px (or any other value) concept from https://kittygiraudel.com/2020/05/18/using-calc-to-figure-out-optimal-line-height/

headline.$description: cushion relates to the 2 * 2px (or any other value) concept from https://kittygiraudel.com/2020/05/18/using-calc-to-figure-out-optimal-line-height/

body.$description: Baseline grid parameters for body text style

utility.$description: Baseline grid parameters for utility text style

headline.$description: Baseline grid parameters for headline text style

factor.$description: factor relates to the 2ex concept from https://kittygiraudel.com/2020/05/18/using-calc-to-figure-out-optimal-line-height/

treshold.$description: Treshold describes the concept to move from bold to semibold headlines at a certrain treshold (font size 28 and larger)

increment.$description: Increment is the value the letter spacing gets reduced every time a treshhold is reached

treshold.$description: Letter spacing treshold is a concept to decrease the letter spacing every 36 font sizes by an increment

s.$description: Mostly used for tools like Figma since the outline border radius is normally calculated in the browser.

m.$description: Mostly used for tools like Figma since the outline border radius is normally calculated in the browser.

l.$description: Mostly used for tools like Figma since the outline border radius is normally calculated in the browser.

all.$description: Current breakpoint name for usage in prototyping tools like Figma

clamp-smaller.$description: Current breakpoint name for usage in prototyping tools like Figma

clamp-larger.$description: Current breakpoint name for usage in prototyping tools like Figma

s.$description: Content size S matches semantic utility line-height S and allows to align component dimensions, e.g. text-icon combination

m.$description: Content size M matches semantic utility line-height M and allows to align component dimensions, e.g. text-icon combination, badge, checkbox, switch or radio-button

l.$description: Content size L matches semantic utility line-height L and allows to align component dimensions, e.g. accordion item content

800.$description: Component sizes define a uniform height system to achieve a more consistent layout structure. Size 800 is used e.g for the button, tag or the avatar.

1000.$description: Component sizes define a uniform height system to achieve a more consistent layout structure. Size 1000 is used e.g. for illustrative icons or the avatar.

1200.$description: Component sizes define a uniform height system to achieve a more consistent layout structure. Size 1200 is used e.g. for the button, input field, dropdown or the avatar.

1400.$description: Component sizes define a uniform height system to achieve a more consistent layout structure. Size 1400 is used e.g. for the illustrative icon.

1600.$description: Component sizes define a uniform height system to achieve a more consistent layout structure. Size 1600 is used e.g. for the avatar or illustrative contained icon.

1800.$description: Component sizes define a uniform height system to achieve a more consistent layout structure.

2000.$description: Component sizes define a uniform height system to achieve a more consistent layout structure. Size 2000 is used e.g. for the avatar or illustrative contained icon.

s.$description: Functional icons need a visual adjustment (reduction of inset) when placed inside component alongside text (icons have an invisible bounding box).

m.$description: Functional icons need a visual adjustment (reduction of inset) when placed inside component alongside text (icons have an invisible bounding box).

default.$description: Helper to provide the grid inset value based on the current breakpoint

functional.$description: Helper to provide the grid inset value based on the current breakpoint

s.$description: Gap between text (e.g. text and icon) for elements without surroundings (e.g. link)

m.$description: Gap between text (e.g. text and icon) for elements without surroundings (e.g. link)

s.$description: Gap between text (e.g. text and icon) for elements with surroundings (e.g. button)

m.$description: Gap between text (e.g. text and icon) for elements with surroundings (e.g. button)

default.$description: Helper to provide the grid gap value based on the current breakpoint

functional.$description: Helper to provide the grid gap value based on the current breakpoint

focus.$description: Distance of the focus ring to the component

content.$description: Max-width for the content area of a website. Use from breakpoint 2xl on and larger, centered horizontally.
